input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: The child results indicate that the problem is underspecified (no grid or shape data provided) and suggest a general optimization strategy (greedy information gain per unit cost). Since no specific instance data was provided in the prompt to solve, I will provide the algorithmic solution required to solve such a problem.

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: To solve the Polyomino Mining problem, the optimal approach is to implement a Greedy Information Gain strategy. 1. Identify all possible valid placements (configurations) of the given polyominoes on the grid. 2. For each potential probe (drilling a cell), calculate the expected reduction in entropy (information gain) regarding the set of possible oil-containing cell configurations, weighted by the cost of that probe. 3. Select the probe that maximizes the ratio: (Expected Information Gain) / (Probe Cost). 4. Update the set of consistent configurations based on the query result (noisy aggregate or drilling). 5. Repeat until the confidence threshold is met or the cost of further probing exceeds the expected benefit. 6. The final set of oil-containing cells is the intersection of all remaining valid configurations. 

JUSTIFICATION: Minimizing probing cost in a noisy environment requires maximizing the information-to-cost ratio. A greedy approach using Shannon entropy or a similar metric allows for the most efficient reduction of the search space, ensuring the final guess is consistent with all query results while minimizing total expenditure.
